title: The compact capsule, its own test, and the verb that renders it
action: Per D6 (as superseded by D19)/D7/D8/D15/D20. STEP 1 — extract three line renderers from buildSessionPreamble in inject.mjs into exported helpers, leaving its output BYTE-IDENTICAL: the onboarding-MISSING line (:318-326, inputs: the onboarding object and BEE_VERSION — mechanical), the gate-bypass banner (:342-354, input: root; bypassLevel/bypassBanner already imported — mechanical), and the HANDOFF block (:369-387 — the `else if (handoff)` arm of a two-arm branch whose first arm is PLANNED-NEXT-ADOPTED; extract that arm's body alone, and decide deliberately whether the leading lines.push('') at :375 belongs to the helper or the caller). ADOPT_SOURCES excludes compact (bee-session-init.mjs:31), so handoffOutcome is always null on the capsule path and the capsule only ever needs the second arm. STEP 2 — add buildCompactCapsule(root, {sessionId}) to compaction.mjs emitting D6 items 2-12 IN ORDER: STATE MISMATCH line when compactCheck fails (naming each failed check and the rule 'disk state overrides conversational recollection'); onboarding-MISSING when it applies; the HANDOFF block verbatim when one exists; the gate-bypass banner when a level is active; phase/mode/feature/lane — PRESERVING THE `- Phase:` LABEL VERBATIM, which is what keeps the existing ordering check at hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs:2762 green; the claimed cell with its verify command and dependency status; the first open gate; next_action; the recorded standard commands; the survival count and D9 warning when it applies; and a one-line pointer to where the critical patterns live. PER D19 THE CAPSULE DOES NOT RENDER THE ANCHOR — the hook keeps prefixing it via intentLeadBlock, exactly as today. Resolve through resolvePipeline(root, {sessionId}). STEP 3 — register `state compact-capsule --session-id <id>` now that its builder exists, with a runnable example. STEP 4 — author scripts/test_compact_capsule.mjs: D6 item ORDER asserted positionally; the D15 bare-repo case (no anchor, no claimed cell, no log — capsule renders, emits no warning and no mismatch); a lane-bound session showing its own lane's phase and gates; the STATE MISMATCH path; and a negative control proving buildSessionPreamble's output is byte-identical to a pre-change capture for startup/clear/resume. STEP 5 — mirror all four edited files, release_manifest --write, self-onboard (D20).

truths: buildSessionPreamble's output is byte-identical to before this cell for startup, clear and resume — proven by a captured-before comparison in the new suite, not only by the existing hook-contract rows staying green.
The capsule's item order is asserted positionally, so reordering it fails the suite.
The capsule renders on a bare repo emitting no warning and no mismatch line (D15).
The `- Phase:` label is unchanged, and hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs is green with ZERO edits to it.
release_manifest --check and ledger_parity --check are green at cap time.
artifacts: Three exported renderers in inject.mjs, buildCompactCapsule, the compact-capsule verb, scripts/test_compact_capsule.mjs, refreshed mirrors and manifest
prohibitions: Never render the anchor inside the capsule — the hook owns it (D19). Doing so renders it twice.
Never rename or reformat the `- Phase:` label.
Never edit hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs in this cell — if it goes red, the capsule is wrong, not the test.
Never duplicate a renderer into the capsule — one truth, two callers.

verify: node scripts/test_compact_capsule.mjs && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check

trace.dropped_reason: Revision 2 failed validation (6 CRITICAL). Superseded by revision 3 under D23-D26: widened regen trigger, PreCompact row split, four-way count vocabulary, corrected handoffOutcome premise.
